questions.question: Explain Tom Mitchell's formal definition of machine learning with an example.
keyPoints: T = Task: what to learn (e.g., classifying emails)
P = Performance measure: how to evaluate (e.g., accuracy)
E = Experience: training data (e.g., labeled emails)
System improves P on T through E
Example: spam filter improves accuracy by learning from labeled emails

questions.question: Differentiate between inductive and deductive learning with examples.
keyPoints: Inductive: specific examples to general rules
Deductive: general rules to specific conclusions
Inductive not guaranteed but practically useful

questions.question: What is the inductive learning hypothesis and why is it important?
keyPoints: Hypothesis that works on training set will work on unseen data
Justifies using training data to build predictive models

questions.question: Compare rote learning and generalization-based learning in the context of ML.
keyPoints: Rote learning: exact memorization, lookup table
Generalization: learns patterns applicable to new data
ML goal is generalization, not memorization
Rote learning fails on unseen examples
